titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the Board of Health and Care Inspectorate's (IVO) handling of individual complaints regarding healthcare services.
html:
In this document, the government presents its assessment of the observations and the recommendation made to the government by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in the report "IVO's handling of individual complaints regarding healthcare" (RiR 2025:29).

The Swedish National Audit Office's overall conclusion is that the Swedish Inspectorate for Healthcare and Welfare's (IVO) handling of individual complaints is not efficient. The Swedish National Audit Office makes a recommendation to the government and a number of recommendations to IVO.

The government welcomes the Swedish National Audit Office's review and believes that the report makes an important contribution to the work of ensuring a fair and effective oversight of healthcare. The government agrees with the Swedish National Audit Office's observations and conclusions. In response to the recommendation made by the Swedish National Audit Office, the government has given IVO a mandate to develop a plan for managing the agency's processing times and caseloads, regarding, for example, individual complaints.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's review report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Office has found that the authority IVO is not effective in handling complaints from patients. The government has now tasked IVO with developing a plan to become faster and more efficient in its work.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors whether other agencies are doing their jobs correctly. They have reviewed the Swedish Inspectorate for Healthcare and Welfare, often abbreviated as IVO. IVO is the agency responsible for ensuring that healthcare functions properly and for receiving complaints from people who are dissatisfied with their care.

The results of the audit show that IVO is not working efficiently when handling complaints from individuals. This means that the process is not working as well as it should when people report problems in healthcare. The Swedish National Audit Office has therefore made recommendations on how things need to change.

The government agrees that there are problems. To address this, the government has given IVO an important task. The agency must now develop a plan on how to reduce its waiting times and ensure that it can handle all the cases that are submitted to it.

With this decision, the government believes that the work on this report is complete. The goal is to ensure that everyone who seeks help or complains about healthcare can trust that the agency is doing a fair and good job.
